task_completion.description: Open and read the source text file containing the glossary terms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit or nano) or a terminal window displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Documents/glossary_terms.txt'.

task_completion.description: Create a new document in the ReqView project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new document appears in the ReqView project tree (left sidebar) or a new document tab is opened in the main workspace.

task_completion.description: Set the document Name to 'Glossary' and ID to 'GLOSS'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ReqView UI shows the document name as 'Glossary' and its ID prefix as 'GLOSS' (visible in the document properties, tab header, or project tree).

task_completion.description: Create five distinct items/rows within the Glossary document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main ReqView document view shows five separate requirement/information rows created under the Glossary document.

task_completion.description: Populate the 'Heading' field of each item with the glossary terms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Heading' column or field for the five items contains the terms: 'MQTT', 'Broker', 'Topic', 'Publish', and 'Subscribe'.

task_completion.description: Populate the 'Description' field of each item with the corresponding definitions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Description' column or text area for each of the five items contains the correct definition matching its heading (e.g., 'Message Queuing Telemetry Transport...' for MQTT).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the ReqView GUI to create the document and items.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the ReqView interface, opening menus, and typing into the application, rather than using terminal commands to write a JSON file directly to the project folder.

integrity.description: Verify the agent correctly mapped terms to Headings and definitions to Descriptions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terms and definitions must be in their respective distinct columns/fields in ReqView, not pasted together as a single block of text in one row or in the document's root description.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the exact definitions provided in the source file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The definitions entered into ReqView must match the text from the source file exactly, indicating the agent didn't use an LLM's internal knowledge to generate alternative definitions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target ReqView document must have the Name 'Glossary' and the ID 'GLOSS'. The source file contains exactly 5 terms to be mapped as Heading -> Description. 1. MQTT -> Message Queuing Telemetry Transport, a lightweight messaging protocol. 2. Broker -> A server that receives all messages from clients and then routes the messages to the appropriate destination clients. 3. Topic -> A string used by the broker to filter messages for each connected client. 4. Publish -> The action of sending a message to a topic. 5. Subscribe -> The action of requesting messages from a topic.
